body.title: pe_chain HYPOTHESIZE: validate-then-retry on bad old_string — LLM paraphrases instead of copying verbatim
body.result: Added _HYPOTHESIZE_MAX_RETRIES=2 and _build_retry_prompt helper. When validation fails on parsed edits, pe_hypothesize re-prompts with the failed attempt + validation error + verbatim-copy reminder. Up to 2 retries; on exhaustion, existing ESCALATE preserved. 105 pe_chain tests pass.
body.description:
Cert walk-02 substitute (T-cc-queue-claim-honors-worker) attempt 1 caught the next engram failure: HYPOTHESIZE LLM produces structurally-correct edit intent but textually wrong old_string. Igor proposed `if t['status'] == 'pending':` — single quotes + ==, where actual code is `if t["status"] != "pending":` — double quotes + !=. The prompt rule 'old_string must appear verbatim' was ignored.

Fix: validate-then-retry pattern (Aider/Cursor convention). When validation fails AND the LLM produced edits, retry up to N=2 with feedback that includes:
- The original prompt (so the LLM still has the ticket + actual code)
- The failed edit's old_string/new_string (so the LLM sees what it produced)
- The validation error (so the LLM knows what to correct)
- Explicit instruction: copy verbatim, including quote style, whitespace, operators

If all retries exhaust, hypothesis_error is set and pe_chain ESCALATES as before (existing behavior preserved).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py — adds _HYPOTHESIZE_MAX_RETRIES const, _build_retry_prompt helper, retry loop in pe_hypothesize.
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN — retry-with-feedback for old_string verbatim failures. OUT — retry on structural parse failures (separate codepath); fuzzy-match auto-correction; switching LLM models.
**Test plan:** existing 105 pe_chain tests pass. Live integration test: cert walk-02 substitute attempt 4 — expected outcome is either validated edit (success) or all-retries-exhausted-with-clearer-error (better diagnostic than before).
